Introducing Universal Dictation on Stream
Push-to-talk across iOS and Mac: Instantly. No app switching, no reconnection.
We built Notes so nothing gets lost, and Chat so you can think out loud. With Dictation, your voice goes anywhere.
Notes, Chat, Dictation—in one voice ring, by Sandbar

results.title: Dictation Software – Disability Resources and Educational Services
results.content:
## Free Dictation Alternatives

### Microsoft Dictate

Platform: Microsoft 365.  
How to get it: Dictate is part of Microsoft’s Immersive Reader tool suite, which supports Universal Design for Learning. It comes built into Word, OneNote, and Outlook.

### Google Docs Voice Typing

Platform: Chrome browser.  
How to get it: Voice Typing comes built into Google Docs in Chrome.

### Windows Speech

Platform: Windows.  
How to get it: Speech comes built into Windows.

### Apple Dictation

Platforms: Mac, iOS.  
How to get it: Dictation comes built into Mac and iOS.

### Gboard keyboard

Platforms: Android, iOS.  
How to get it: Download Gboard here.

## For More Information [...] ### Who can benefit?

Below is an example of a dictation program used at the University of Illinois, as well as some free alternatives available to anyone. The free alternatives listed are by no means exhaustive, and many won’t have all the features available from paid programs. However, they do give a good example of the basic functionalities available, and may help determine whether it’s worth pursuing more feature-rich alternatives. [...] ## Dragon NatuallySpeaking

Platforms: Windows, Android.

Dragon is among the best-selling dictation programs on the market, and consistently rates among the most accurate in terms of speech recognition. Dragon NaturallySpeaking can also be trained to your voice, increasing accuracy the more you use it. It’s available to DRES students with a speech-to-text accommodation during exams taken in the Testing Accommodation Center, as well as on computers at the Beckwith Residential Support Services at Nugent Hall.

### Feature Highlights

### See it in action

Dragon offers demo videos that showcase its features and offer tips and tricks for better dictation. Their Dictation video gives a good overview of what NaturallySpeaking looks like in action.

## Free Dictation Alternatives

## What to Consider

## Wispr Flow

Wispr Flow

Speak naturally, write perfectly & 4x faster in every app

### Best for

Best for cross-device knowledge workers who want system-wide dictation and strong multilingual code-switching.

### Standout features

## Willow Voice

Willow Voice

Voice dictation that actually works

### Best for [...] Voice dictation that actually works

### Best for

Ideal for professionals who write all day in email and chat and want instant, polished dictation with minimal setup.

### Standout features

## Aqua Voice

Aqua Voice

Fast Voice Input for Mac and Windows

### Best for

Best for developers and heavy writers who need screen-aware, long-form dictation on desktop.

### Standout features

## superwhisper

superwhisper

Extremely accurate, voice to text for Mac & iPhone

### Best for

Ideal for privacy-conscious power users who want offline dictation and customizable voice workflows.

### Standout features

## AudioPen

AudioPen

The easiest way to convert messy thoughts into clear text

results.content: with it. For even longer files, you probably have to do some tests, and you probably will want to do everything with Keyboard Maestro and skip the shortcuts app totally. But that's beyond the simple alternative that I wanted to share here. As I'm wrapping up this video, I just want to tell you, if you want the absolutely best that I have tried so far, there's Super Whisperer. Also, a few months ago, I made an Alfred workflow called Kiki, and the reason that I'm so familiar with this topic is because Kiki incorporates a similar functionality to Super Whisperer. You can do a lot of the same things. The problem is, well, it's not super easy to set up. There's a long documentation, and maybe in the future I will make some more tutorials for it. But unless you know how to deal with AppleScript
